# Copilot Instructions for YT Shortless

## Project Overview
YT Shortless is a minimal Android app that loads `https://m.youtube.com` in a WebView and hides all YouTube Shorts-related UI elements using injected CSS and a MutationObserver. It has no backend, no REST API, and no local databaseâ€”just a single-Activity Android app.

## Tech Stack
- **Language**: Kotlin
- **Platform**: Android (minSdk 24, targetSdk 34, compileSdk 34)
- **Build system**: Gradle (Groovy DSL), wrapper included â€” no global Gradle install needed
- **JVM**: Java 17 (use Android Studio's bundled JDK or set `JAVA_HOME` accordingly)
- **Key dependencies**: `androidx.core:core-ktx`, `androidx.appcompat:appcompat`, `com.google.android.material:material`
- **No test framework** is currently configured

## Project Structure
```
app/
  src/main/
    java/com/example/ytshortless/
      MainActivity.kt        # Single activity: WebView setup, CSS injection, fullscreen
    res/
      layout/activity_main.xml
      values/strings.xml, themes.xml
      mipmap-*/              # Launcher icons (all densities)
    AndroidManifest.xml
  build.gradle               # App-level build config, versioning, signing tasks
build.gradle                 # Root build config
gradle.properties            # Version numbers (VERSION_MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH), Kotlin style
keystore.properties.example  # Template for release signing (never commit actual secrets)
apk/                         # Versioned APK archive for sharing/testing
```

## Build Commands

### Debug build (Linux/macOS)
```bash
./gradlew :app:assembleDebug
# Output: app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk
```

### Debug build (Windows PowerShell)
```powershell
$env:JAVA_HOME="C:\Program Files\Android\Android Studio\jbr"
$env:Path="$env:JAVA_HOME\bin;$env:Path"
.\gradlew.bat :app:assembleDebug
```

### Release build (requires `keystore.properties`)
```bash
./gradlew :app:assembleRelease
# Output: app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk
```

### Archive release APK (required after every release build)
After each successful `assembleRelease`, copy the generated APK into the `apk/` folder using the versioned naming format:

- `apk/yt-shortless-<versionName>.apk`

Windows PowerShell example:
```powershell
$version = (& .\gradlew.bat -q :app:printVersion | Select-String 'versionName=').ToString().Split('=')[1].Trim()
$src = 'app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk'
$dst = "apk/yt-shortless-$version.apk"
if (Test-Path $dst) { Remove-Item $dst -Force }
Copy-Item -Path $src -Destination $dst -Force
```

### Utility tasks
```bash
./gradlew :app:printVersion          # Print current versionName and versionCode
./gradlew :app:validateReleaseSigning # Validate keystore.properties before release
```

## Versioning
- Follows Semantic Versioning (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`).
- Update `VERSION_MAJOR`, `VERSION_MINOR`, `VERSION_PATCH` in `gradle.properties`.
- `versionCode` = `MAJOR * 10000 + MINOR * 100 + PATCH`.
- Document every release in `CHANGELOG.md` before tagging.
- Changelog entries must describe real user-visible changes/fixes; avoid redundant bullets that only restate the release version/versionCode.
- Archive every release APK in `apk/` with versioned filename right after building.

## Coding Conventions
- Follow the **official Kotlin code style** (`kotlin.code.style=official` in `gradle.properties`).
- Keep all Android logic in `MainActivity.kt`; avoid creating new Activities unless the feature genuinely requires one.
- CSS selectors for hiding Shorts live in `injectShortsHidingCss()` â€” add new selectors there if YouTube updates its DOM.
- The JS escape helper `escapeForJs()` must be used whenever injecting Kotlin strings into JavaScript to avoid injection issues.
- Do not enable `minifyEnabled` for release builds unless ProGuard rules are also updated.
- Never commit `keystore.properties` or `release-keystore.jks`; they are listed in `.gitignore`.

## Release Signing
- Copy `keystore.properties.example` â†’ `keystore.properties` and fill in the values.
- The keystore file path relative to the project root is set via `storeFile` (e.g., `storeFile=../release-keystore.jks`).
- Keep the keystore backed up; losing it prevents future updates under the same package name.

## Icons
- Adaptive icon foreground/background source PNGs live in `mipmap-xxxhdpi` (432Ă—432 px).
- All other densities are auto-generated by Android Studio's Image Asset tool.
- Play Store icon (512Ă—512) is at `assets/branding/play-store-icon-512.png`.
